title: Republicans Are Lost in the AI Wilderness
description: The Trump administration went all in on artificial intelligence. Then the public started hating it. That’s a big political problem, writes tech columnist John Herrman.
content: Going into the midterms, the bipartisan backlash against AI gives Democrats a number of politically attractive options. They can try to tap into the growing opposition to data centers, particularly in states where the issue has become suddenly and ma... [8112 chars]

title: AI may be fuelling U.S. business creation, but few signs of a similar trend in Canada
description: Wider adoption of artificial intelligence south of the border is coinciding with an uptick in new business formation
content: Artificial intelligence is supposed to make everything easier, including starting a business. Chatbots can serve as advisers, while AI applications can speed up many core functions of a business. Tech start-ups can grow faster with fewer employees an... [7474 chars]

title: Rajasthan Farmers Get AI Tools For Crop Health And Real Time Weather Updates
description: The Rajasthan Agriculture Department has partnered with Wadhwani AI to provide 95 lakh farmers with real time updates and artificial intelligence solutions for crop diseases, weather changes, and irrigation management.

title: Japan warmed up to AI late but that could turn out to be an advantage as this technology evolves
description: The US and China dominate artificial intelligence (AI), be it development or diffusion. But Japan, once a global tech leader, could benefit by learning from the mistakes of AI pioneers. Slow and steady could work even in an arena like this.
content: Diffusion, meanwhile, is finally picking up. During the first quarter this year, AI adoption in Japan rose 3.4 percentage points, over three times faster than the global average, according to Microsoft’s AI Economy Institute Q1 report. Separate data ... [3685 chars]
